definition: Initialism of United States Department of Veterans Affairs.
To move out of a dwelling or other property, either by choice or by eviction.
An extended trip or journey away from home for rest or pleasure.
Of, pertaining to, caused by, or characteristic of cowpox.
A region of space that contains no matter.
A person on a trip of indeterminate destination and/or length of time.
Not clearly expressed; stated in indefinite terms.
Initialism of verb animate intransitive: an intransitive verb that agrees with an animate subject.
Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.
Possessing or showing courage or determination; brave, heroic.
The state of being valid, authentic or genuine.
A piece of hand luggage such as a suitcase or travelling bag.
An elongated depression cast between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it.
The quality that renders something desirable or valuable; worth.
A device that controls the flow of a gas or fluid through a space, such as a pipe, manifold, or plenum.

A covered motor vehicle used to carry goods or (normally less than ten) persons, usually roughly cuboid in shape, Depending on the type of van, it can be bigger or smaller than a pickup truck and SUV, and longer and higher than a car but relatively smaller than a truck/lorry or a bus.
Willful damage to or destruction of any property, such as graffiti or defacement.
The leading units at the front of an army or fleet.
Any tropical climbing orchid of the genus Vanilla (especially Vanilla planifolia), bearing podlike fruit yielding an extract used in flavoring food or in perfumes.
To become invisible or to move out of view unnoticed.
That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.
Able to vary or be varied.
The act of varying; a partial change in the form, position, state, or qualities of a thing.
simple past and past participle of vary
Streaked, spotted, or otherwise marked with a variety of color.
More than one (of an indeterminate set of things).
A clear or translucent liquid coating composed of resin dissolved in a solvent, which dries by evaporation to form a hard, protective, and typically glossy finish.
To change with time or a similar parameter.
A vessel or duct transporting any bodily fluid, such as blood, lymph, chyle, or semen.
An upright open container used mainly for displaying fresh, dried, or artificial flowers.
Very large or wide (literally or figuratively).
A large tub, such as is used for making wine or for tanning.
An arched masonry structure supporting and forming a ceiling, whether freestanding or forming part of a larger building.
The name of the Cyrillic script letter В / в.
The flesh of a calf (i.e. a young bovine) used for food.
A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
Capable of growth and reproduction, but not feeling or reason (often opposed to sensible and rational).
A person who does not eat animal flesh, or, in some cases, use any animal products.
Showing strong feelings; passionate; forceful or intense.
A conveyance: a spacious device for carrying or transporting substances, objects, people or animals.
Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar diaphanous material.
A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.
A vector quantity that denotes the rate of change of position with respect to time, combining speed with a directional component.
A closely woven fabric (originally of silk, now also of cotton or man-made fibres) with a thick short pile on one side.
Synonym of sell, (now especially) to sell through a vending machine.
A person or a company that vends or sells.
Commanding respect because of age, dignity, character or position.
Revenge taken for an insult, injury, or other wrong.
An animal toxin intended for defensive or offensive use; a biological poison delivered by bite, sting, etc., to protect an animal or to kill its prey.

Of an animal (specifically a snake) or parts of its body: producing venom (“a toxin intended for defensive or offensive use”) which is usually injected into an enemy or prey by biting or stinging; hence, of a bite or sting: injecting venom.
An opening through which gases, especially air, can pass.
Larger than grande, usually 20 ounces (~ 0.6 l).
To replace stale or noxious air with fresh.
The replacement of stale or noxious air with fresh.
A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
One of the ancient transverse flutes of Indian classical music.
A theater, auditorium, arena, or other area designated for sporting or entertainment events.
A gallery, platform, or balcony, usually roofed and often partly enclosed, extending along the outside of a building.
A word that indicates an action, event, or state of being.
A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.
A rod or staff of office, e.g. of a verger.
The act or process of verifying.
To substantiate or prove the truth of something.
The language of a people or a national language.
Willing to take either a penetrative (top) or receptive (bottom) role in anal sex.
Capable of doing many things competently.
A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.
A specific form or variation of something.
The back side of a flat object which is to be examined visually, as for reading, such as a sheet, leaf, coin or medal;
A stance that attempts to understand the meaning of action from the actor’s point of view, so that the actor is seen as a subject rather than an object of observation.
A green colour, now only in heraldry; represented in engraving by diagonal parallel lines 45 degrees counter-clockwise.
Standing, pointing, or moving straight up or down; parallel to the local direction of gravity; along the direction of a plumb line; perpendicular to something horizontal.
The fine arts as a subject of study or expertise; understanding of arts and antiquities.
To a great extent or degree.
A craft for transportation on or in water, air, or space.
A sleeveless garment that buttons down the front, worn over a shirt, and often as part of a suit; a waistcoat.
Settled, fixed or absolute, with no contingencies.
A mark left on the earth by a foot.
A person with long experience of a particular activity.
A medical doctor who treats non-human animals.
A political right to disapprove of (and thereby stop) the process of a decision, a law etc.
Initialism of verb intransitive or intransitive verb, often in dictionaries.
A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
Able to live on its own (as for a newborn).
To shake with small, rapid movements to and fro.
The act of vibrating or the condition of being vibrated.
In the Church of England, the priest of a parish, receiving a salary or stipend but not tithes.
Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
Proximity; the state of being near.
One that is harmed—killed, injured, subjected to oppression, deceit, or otherwise adversely affected—by someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular
To make (someone) a victim or sacrifice.
The winner in a fight or contest.
Being the winner in a contest, struggle, war, etc.
The condition or state of having won a battle or competition, or having succeeded in an effort; (countable) an instance of this.
Television, a television show, or a movie.
A relatively wide magnetic tape used for recording visual images and sounds (as of a television production) for subsequent playback or broadcasting.
To fight for superiority; to contend; to compete eagerly so as to gain something.
Someone who views a spectacle; an onlooker or spectator.
The position from which something is observed or considered
An instance of keeping awake during normal sleeping hours, especially to keep watch or pray.
Watchful, especially for danger or disorder; alert; wary
A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.
A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.
A person who lives in, or comes from, a village.
To clear of an accusation, suspicion or criticism.
An act of asserting or maintaining; an assertion.
Having a tendency to seek revenge when wronged, vengeful.
Any plant whose stem requires support and which climbs by tendrils or twining.
A sour liquid formed by the fermentation of alcohol used as a condiment or preservative; a dilute solution of acetic acid.
The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.
To break or disregard (a rule or convention).
The act or an instance of violating or the condition of being violated.
A plant or flower of the genus Viola, especially the fragrant Viola odorata; (inexact) similar-looking plants and flowers.
A small unfretted stringed instrument with four strings tuned (lowest to highest) G-D-A-E, usually held against the chin and played with a bow.
A person who plays the violin.
A kind of organ stop producing a string-like sound.
A person who has never had sexual intercourse, or (uncommonly) an animal that has never mated.
Manly; having characteristics associated with being male, such as strength; exhibiting masculine traits to an exaggerated degree such as strength, forcefulness or vigor.
In effect or essence, rather than in fact or reality; also, imitated, simulated.

(uncountable) The idea of all that is good or excellent (in every sense of those terms) in a human being, collectively instantiated by a varying number of human traits known as "the virtues", the enumeration of which vary by the many virtue systems which have developed within different cultures, religions, and historical periods.
Full of virtue; having excellent moral character.

A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure that consists of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and that sometimes causes disease in the host organism (such agents are often classed as nonliving infectious particles and less often as microorganisms).
A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.
The sense or ability of sight.
To habitually go to (someone in distress, sickness etc.) to comfort them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
The chapel at the center of a barrio.
A light cape or short cloak made of silk or lace, formerly worn by women in summer.
Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.
A distant view or prospect, especially one seen through some opening, avenue or passage.
Related to or affecting the vision.
Relating to or characteristic of life.
The capacity to live and develop.

Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
Long live ... ! (used to express acclaim or support).
Lively and animated; full of life and energy.
A usually alphabetized and explained collection of words e.g. of a particular field, or prepared for a specific purpose, often for learning.
Of, pertaining to, or resembling the human voice or speech.
A divine calling to establish one's lifestyle.
Of or pertaining to a vocation.
Making or characterized by a noisy outcry; clamorous.
A town in Taita-Taveta County, southern Kenya.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character.
Containing nothing; empty; not occupied or filled.
Lo, there it is; ta-da; presto; behold!
A carriage; a vehicle, now chiefly in French contexts.
Evaporating or vaporizing readily under normal conditions.
Of or pertaining to a volcano or volcanoes.

A vent or fissure on the surface of a planet (usually in a mountainous form) with a magma chamber attached to the mantle of a planet or moon, periodically erupting forth lava and volcanic gases onto the surface.
A town and municipality in Tumbaya department, Jujuy province, Argentina.
The simultaneous firing of a number of missiles or bullets; the projectiles so fired.
A game played on a rectangular court between two teams of two to six players which involves striking a ball back and forth over a net.
The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
Fluent or having a ready flow of speech.
A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
Done, given, or acting of one's own free will.
One who enters into, or offers themselves for, any service of their own free will, especially when done without pay.
Characterized or marked by full, generous, pleasurable sensation.
To regurgitate or eject the contents of the stomach through the mouth; puke.
A prior version or manifestation of a text.
Initialism of verb–object–subject: a language is VOS if a typical sentence starts with the verb, followed by the object, followed by the subject.
A formalized choice on legally relevant measures such as employment or appointment to office or a proceeding about a legal dispute.
A commune of Indre-et-Loire department, France.
To call on (someone) to be a witness to something.
A piece of paper or other token that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
A solemn promise to perform some act, or behave in a specified manner, especially a promise to live and act in accordance with the rules of a religious order.
A sound produced by the vocal cords with relatively little restriction of the oral cavity, forming the prominent sound of a syllable.
A long journey, especially by ship.
More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
Any of several carrion-eating birds of the families Accipitridae and Cathartidae.
